key_insight: An AI-ECG “age gap” measure can identify accelerated cardiac aging and is strongly associated with higher future cardiovascular event risk.
clinical_relevance: This non-invasive digital biomarker could help stratify cardiovascular risk across hospital and community populations before overt disease develops.
method: Trained an ECGFounder-based AI model on healthy UK Biobank ECGs to predict biological cardiac age.
Validated the model in independent clinical and external inpatient/community cohorts.
Used Cox proportional hazards models to test links between ECG age gap and cardiovascular outcomes.
evidence_signal: In UK Biobank, predicted age aligned moderately with calendar age (r = 0.55; MAE = 5.12 years), and each 1-year increase in age gap was associated with a 13% higher MACCE risk (HR 1.13, 95% CI 1.11-1.14).
watch_item: The age-gap metric shows strong association, but real-world use will need prospective impact testing, calibration across settings, and clear thresholds to avoid misclassification.
paper_title: AI-Derived ECG Age Gap as a Digital Biomarker for Cardiovascular Risk: External Validation in Hospital and Community-Based Prospective Cohorts
